intro: Python relies heavily on procedural code or explicit runtime objects for data processing pipeline development. Moving to Scala shifts development toward a powerful hybrid functional/object-oriented paradigm backed by a highly sophisticated static type system. This switch is typically made for low-latency streaming applications, complex data processing engineering, or within large-scale Apache Spark architectures.

diffs.to: Deep integration of object-oriented and purely functional programming.

diffs.from: Dynamic; type annotations are purely optional metadata hints.
diffs.to: Static, highly complex type inference system with dependent types.

diffs.from: Mutable by default; explicit creation of immutable copies.
diffs.to: Immutable collections by default, optimizing safe parallel execution.

diffs.from: Process separation or threading bounded by the GIL.
diffs.to: Actor models, Futures, and functional effect systems (e.g., ZIO).
First-class pattern matching on case classes and type patterns.

examples.title: Pattern Matching
examples.description: Evaluating data structures structurally based on type or values.
examples.fromCode:
def process(payload):
    match payload:
        case {"status": "success", "code": c}:
            return f"Ok: {c}"
        case _:
            return "Error"

examples.title: Immutable Transformation
examples.description: Applying stateless sequence mapping across data collections.
examples.fromCode:
items = [1, 2, 3, 4]
result = [x * 10 for x in items]
examples.toCode:
val items = List(1, 2, 3, 4)
val result = items.map(_ * 10)

tips.title: Favor Immutability
tips.body: Construct processing steps using immutable collections and "val" references to eliminate side effects.

tips.title: Master Implicit Parameters
tips.body: Understand Scala implicit parameters (or context parameters in Scala 3) for handling configurations.

tips.title: Return Expressions
tips.body: Omit explicit return statements; the final evaluated block expression is automatically returned in Scala.

tips.title: Handle Missing Values via Option
tips.body: Represent optional values using Option (Some/None) instead of passing or checking for null.
